items.title: Michel Bitbol - Why is the Quantum so Strange?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content: <article>To know reality, one must confront the quantum. It is how our world works at the deepest level. What’s the quantum? It is bizarre, defying all common sense. Particles in two positions at the same time. Spooky action at a distance. It would sound absurd if it weren’t true.</article>

items.title: Andrew Torrance - Can the Bible Address Philosophical Questions?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Listen to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts. Subscribe today: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Philosophy of religion poses numerous questions about God: “proof” or “disproofs” of existence, nature and attributes, personhood, relation to time, relation to humankind—it’s a long list. In Judeo-Christian religions, the ultimate authority is supposed to be the Bible. But the Bible seems radically different from philosophy of religion.

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

Alan Torrance is professor of systematic theology at St Mary’s College of the University of St Andrews. He is also Co-Director of the Logos Institute for Analytic and Exegetical Theology, alongside Oliver Crisp.

Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

items.title: Clément Vidal on Fermi’s Paradox and Alien Intelligence | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Do intelligent aliens exist? In perhaps his boldest hypothesis, Clément Vidal argues that signs of advanced extraterrestrial civilizations are already present in our astrophysical data.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

Dr. Clément Vidal is a philosopher with a background in logic and cognitive sciences. He is co-director of the 'Evo Devo Universe' community and founder of the 'High Energy Astrobiology' prize. To satisfy his intellectual curiosity when facing the big questions, he brings together many areas of knowledge such as cosmology, physics, astrobiology, complexity science, evolutionary theory and philosophy of science.</article>

items.title: Alex Gómez-Marín - Transhuman Consciousness?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Support the show with a Closer To Truth merch purchase. Shop T-shirts, mugs, notebooks, hoodies, and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

What happens to consciousness in transhumanism? Would transhuman creatives have different conscious experiences? Would melding with machines change our inner sense of felt experience? How would we know? How would it matter?

Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Alex Gómez-Marín is a Spanish physicist turned neuroscientist. He holds a PhD in theoretical physics and a Masters in biophysics from the University of Barcelona. He was a research fellow at the EMBL-CRG Centre for Genomic Regulation and at the Champalimaud Centre for the Unknown in Lisbon. His research spans from the origins of the arrow of time in inert systems, to the neurobiology of action-perception in living organisms such as flies, worms, mice, and humans.

items.title: Unlocking consciousness: Near-death experiences
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content: <article>Etzel Cardeña discusses near-death experiences, spiritual realities, cultural interpretations, and the distinction between experience and conclusion. #shorts</article>

items.title: Ernan McMullin - Is God the Cause of a Fine-Tuned Universe?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Our universe is astounding in that the laws of physics have to be perfect beyond belief for stars and planets, and for human beings, to even exist. Does this demonstrate, if not prove, an intelligence behind the universe, who many call God? There are other, naturalistic explanations. Can we compare natural and supernatural reasons?

Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/Xsn2z

Ernan McMullin was an Irish philosopher who last served as the O’Hara Professor of Philosophy Emeritus at the University of Notre Dame.

items.title: Silvia Jonas - Is Mathematics Invented or Discovered?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without paywalls: https://shorturl.at/l3q6G

Mathematics describes the real world of atoms and acorns, stars and stairs, with remarkable precision. So is mathematics invented by humans just like chisels and hammers and pieces of music? Or is mathematics discovered—always out there, somewhere, like mysterious islands waiting to be found? Whenever mathematics is will help define reality itself.

Watch more videos on the origin of mathematics here: https://shorturl.at/E8PDp

Silvia Jonas is a Professor of Philosophy at the University of Bamberg, an external member of the Munich Center for Mathematical Philosophy, and an associated member of the Konstanz Center for Philosophy of Mathematics. She also serves on the board of trustees of the Martin Buber Society of Fellows and is a trusted advisor for the Ernst Ludwig Ehrlich Studienwerk.

items.title: Roger Penrose on the Deep Nature of Reality | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

In this first installment of a brand new, six-part interview series with Nobel Laureate Sir Roger Penrose, we explore his approach to the deep nature of reality. Trace the intellectual and scientific history of each of his major contributions to the fields of science and philosophy, the challenges he faced, the criticisms he received, and his overarching reflections today.

Throughout his career, Penrose has challenged conventional wisdom in physics, mathematics, and the philosophy of science. He has tackled some of the most profound questions of the universe: How do quantum mechanics and cosmology shape our understanding of reality? Can human consciousness be fully explained by physical laws, or does it transcend computation? This wide-ranging conversation spans Penrose’s most influential ideas, from black holes and the Big Bang to Gödel’s incompleteness theorems, quantum gravity, and the role of mathematics in uncovering ultimate reality.

Sir Roger Penrose is an English mathematician, mathematical physicist, philosopher of science, and Nobel Laureate in Physics. He is Emeritus Rouse Ball Professor of Mathematics at the University of Oxford, an emeritus fellow of Wadham College, Oxford, and an honorary fellow of St John's College, Cambridge, and University College London. Penrose has contributed significantly to the mathematical physics of general relativity and cosmology.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e</article>

items.title: Can Art Affect Belief Systems? | Episode 2503 | Closer To Truth
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Many belief systems, religions in particular, use art for promoting their messages and furthering their missions. What makes art so effective in building and solidifying religious traditions? Can art attract and motivate converts? Is affecting or changing belief systems a proper use of art?

Featuring interviews with Justin Barrett, Marlene Altenmüller, Mario Gollwitzer, Jamal Elias, Robin Jensen, and Stacie Friend.

items.title: John Bishop - Alternative Concepts of God?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Is God, if there is a God, a personal, conscious, all-powerful Supreme Being? Some offer radically different concepts of ‘God’, exploring novel ideas of what God may be like. They challenge theism—the God of Judaism, Christianity and Islam—with radically new kinds of gods. Is this ‘heresy’? Or enlarging our vision of what the Ultimate can be like?

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

John Bishop is a Professor of Philosophy at the University of Auckland in New Zealand. He hodls a BA from the Australian National Unviersity and a PhD from Cambridge.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

items.title: Alex Gómez-Marín - Idealism: Arguing Pro and Con
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Idealism is the theory that all reality is consciousness and only consciousness, and that what we perceive to be the physical world is either a manifestation of consciousness or derived from it. Either way, fundamental reality is pure consciousness. What are the arguments for and against Idealism (like the common sense of the physical)?

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

Alex Gómez-Marín is a Spanish physicist turned neuroscientist. He holds a PhD in theoretical physics and a Masters in biophysics from the University of Barcelona. He was a research fellow at the EMBL-CRG Centre for Genomic Regulation and at the Champalimaud Centre for the Unknown in Lisbon. His research spans from the origins of the arrow of time in inert systems, to the neurobiology of action-perception in living organisms such as flies, worms, mice, and humans.

items.title: Susan Schneider on Transhumanism | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Dr. Susan Schneider is a philosopher and artificial intelligence (AI) expert. She is the founding director of the Center for the Future Mind at Florida Atlantic University where she also holds the William F. Dietrich Distinguished Professorship. Schneider has also held the Baruch S. Blumberg NASA/Library of Congress Chair in Astrobiology, Exploration, and Scientific Innovation at NASA and the Distinguished Scholar Chair at the Library of Congress. Visit her website at https://www.SchneiderWebsite.com.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

This Chat was made possible by MindFest 2025: "Sentience, Autonomy, and the Future of Human-AI Interaction", a two-day event presented by the Center for the Future Mind.

To learn more about groundbreaking innovation in AI, neuroscience, and the study of consciousness, visit the Center for the Future Mind, view MindFest videos, and subscribe to their newsletter, visit: https://shorturl.at/mYtqz.</article>

items.title: Philip Goff - Consciousness and Life After Death?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Closer To Truth is now on Bluesky! Follow us for updates, announcements, and more: https://shorturl.at/GSR4k

Life after death is explored in the context of diverse theories of consciousness, from strict Materialism/Physicalism to those of ancient wisdom traditions. We explore the view that the haunting and deeply personal question of life after death relates to theories of consciousness.

Make a donation to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Philip Goff is a British author, panpsychist philosopher, and professor at Durham University whose research focuses on philosophy of mind and consciousness. Specifically, it focuses on how consciousness can be part of the scientific worldview.

Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l
